About

Data source details are provided below.

Dataset information

Full dataset name: “Canada’s Energy Future 2023: Energy Supply and Demand Projections to 2050”

Dataset page: https://open.canada.ca/data/en/dataset/7643c948-d661-4d90-ab91-e9ac732fc737

Data source: Open Government (https://open.canada.ca/en)

Record ID: 7643c948-d661-4d90-ab91-e9ac732fc737

Digital Object Identifier (DOI): https://doi.org/10.35002/zppg-yr91

Organization Name: Canada Energy Regulator (CER) / Régie de l’énergie du Canada (REC)

Likely future scenarios

Below is a digest of data trends seen in the dashboard, focusing on 2023-2050 projections and attempting to estimate the scale of corrections required to achieve net-zero targets, as well as their consequences. For example, crude oil total production decreases by about -40% in a global net-zero scenario.

Note: Unless specified otherwise, values listed in pairs refer to Canada Net-zero and Global Net-zero scenarios (respectively).

Production

  • Alberta the top contributor across the board.

  • Crude Oil down (-15 to -40%), with AB producing 82% on average.

  • Hydrogen on the rise (+225 to +219%), with AB producing 40% on average.

  • Natural Gas down (-18 to -39%), with AB producing 58% on average.

Financials

  • Crude Oil prices favor Canadian selling (The WTI-WCS differential1 is reduced by 0 to -10% on average).

  • Natural Gas prices favor Canadian selling significantly (The Henry Hub-NIT differential is reduced by -20 to -44% on average).

  • End-Use Prices ….

  • Macro Indicators ….

Dashboard hierarchy

Dashboard Page
  ├ Overview
  ├ Production
  │ ├ Crude Oil
  │ │ ├ Total
  │ │ ├ (Upgraded Bitumen)
  │ │ ├ C5+
  │ │ ├ Conventional Heavy
  │ │ ├ Conventional Light
  │ │ ├ Field Condensate
  │ │ ├ In Situ Bitumen
  │ │ └ Mined Bitumen
  │ ├ Hydrogen
  │ │ ├ Total (calculated)
  │ │ ├ Biomass
  │ │ ├ Electrolysis
  │ │ └ Natural gas with CCS
  │ └ Natural Gas
  │   ├ Total
  │   ├ Solution
  │   ├ Non Associated
  │   ├ Tight
  │   ├ Shale
  │   └ Coalbed Methane
  ├ Financials
  │ ├ Benchmark Prices
  │ │ ├ Crude Oil
  │ │ └ Natural Gas
  │ ├ End Use Prices
  │ │ ├ ...
  │ │ ├ ...
  │ │ ├ ...
  │ │ └ ...
  │ └ Macro Indicators
  │   └ ...
  └ ...

Notes

Note on tabs: Tabs are sorted by decreasing total quantity (as per value cards). This means the top category is listed second, following Total.

Note on years: Years 2022 (latest current data), 2030 (interim near-net-zero target), 2035 (negotiated interim near-net-zero target as per the Canada-Alberta MOU), and 2050 (target year as per EF2023/the Canadian Net-Zero Emissions Accountability Act) are highlighted.

Navigation hint: Single- or double-click legend items.

Contact:

At any time, requests for data projects are welcome.

These pages were built in R using Quarto.

Footnotes

  1. A common measure of heavy crude oil market viability. The gap between Canadian heavy and U.S. light. A narrow spread indicates high demand for heavy oil, offsetting refinery costs. See also “basis spread” or “basis”.↩︎
